# <span id="page-677-0"></span>**Amazon VPC quotas**

The following tables list the quotas, formerly referred to as limits, for Amazon VPC resources for your AWS account. Unless indicated otherwise, these quotas are per Region.

If you request a quota increase that applies per resource, we increase the quota for all resources in the Region.

# <span id="page-677-1"></span>**VPC and subnets**

| Name                                                                              | Default | Adjustable                                                                                                                              | Comments                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------|---------|-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| VPCs<br>per<br>Region                                                             | 5       | Yes                                                                                                                                     | Increasing<br>this<br>quota<br>increases<br>the quota on internet<br>gateways<br>per<br>Region<br>by<br>the<br>same amount.<br>You<br>can<br>increase<br>this<br>limit<br>so<br>that<br>you<br>can<br>have<br>hundreds<br>of<br>VPCs<br>per<br>Region. |
| Subnets per VPC                                                                   | 200     | Yes                                                                                                                                     |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |
| IPv4 CIDR blocks per VPC                                                          | 5       | Yes<br>(up to 50)                                                                                                                       | This<br>primary<br>CIDR<br>block<br>and<br>all<br>secondary<br>CIDR<br>blocks<br>count<br>toward<br>this<br>quota.                                                                                                                                     |
| IPv6 CIDR blocks per VPC                                                          | 5       | Yes<br>(up to 50)                                                                                                                       | The<br>number<br>of<br>CIDRs<br>you<br>can<br>allocate to a single VPC.                                                                                                                                                                                |
| VPC<br>Block<br>Public<br>Access<br>exclusions<br>per<br>account<br>per<br>Region | 50      | Yes.<br>To<br>request<br>an<br>increase,<br>see<br>Request<br>a<br>service<br>quota<br>increase in<br>the AWS<br>Support<br>User Guide. | The number of VPC<br>BPA<br>exclusions<br>you<br>can<br>create<br>in<br>an<br>account.                                                                                                                                                                 |

VPC and subnets 667

# <span id="page-678-0"></span>**DNS**

Each EC2 instance can send 1024 packets per second per network interface to Route 53 Resolver (specifically the .2 address, such as 10.0.0.2 and 169.254.169.253). This quota cannot be increased. The number of DNS queries per second supported by Route 53 Resolver varies by the type of query, the size of the response, and the protocol in use. For more information and recommendations for a scalable DNS architecture, see the AWS Hybrid DNS with Active [Directory](https://d1.awsstatic.com/whitepapers/aws-hybrid-dns-with-active-directory.pdf) Technical Guide.

# <span id="page-678-1"></span>**Elastic IP addresses**

| Name                                                          | Default | Adjustabl<br>e | Comments                                                                                         |
|---------------------------------------------------------------|---------|----------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Elastic<br>IP<br>addresses<br>per<br>Region                   | 5       | Yes            | This<br>quota<br>applies<br>to<br>individual<br>AWS<br>account<br>VPCs<br>and<br>shared<br>VPCs. |
| Elastic<br>IP<br>addresses<br>per<br>public<br>NAT<br>gateway | 2       | Yes            | You<br>can<br>request<br>a<br>quota<br>increase<br>up<br>to<br>8.                                |

# <span id="page-678-2"></span>**Gateways**

| Name                                                 | Default | Adjustabl<br>e | Comments                                                                                                                                                                                                         |
|------------------------------------------------------|---------|----------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Egress-only<br>internet<br>gateways<br>per<br>Region | 5       | Yes            | To<br>increase<br>this<br>quota,<br>increase<br>the<br>quota<br>for<br>VPCs<br>per<br>Region.<br>You<br>can<br>attach<br>only<br>one<br>egress-only<br>internet<br>gateway<br>to<br>a<br>VPC<br>at<br>a<br>time. |
| Internet<br>gateways<br>per<br>Region                | 5       | Yes            | To<br>increase<br>this<br>quota,<br>increase<br>the<br>quota<br>for<br>VPCs<br>per<br>Region.<br>You<br>can<br>attach<br>only<br>one<br>internet<br>gateway<br>to<br>a<br>VPC<br>at<br>a<br>time.                |

DNS 668

| Name                                                       | Default | Adjustabl<br>e | Comments                                                                                                    |
|------------------------------------------------------------|---------|----------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| NAT<br>gateways<br>per<br>Availabil<br>ity<br>Zone         | 5       | Yes            | NAT<br>gateways<br>only<br>count<br>toward<br>your<br>quota in the pending, active, and<br>deleting states. |
| Private<br>IP<br>address<br>quota<br>per<br>NAT<br>gateway | 8       | Yes            |                                                                                                             |
| Carrier<br>gateways<br>per<br>VPC                          | 1       | No             |                                                                                                             |

# <span id="page-679-0"></span>**Customer-managed prefix lists**

While the default quotas for customer-managed prefix lists are adjustable, you cannot request an increase using the Service Quotas console. You must open a support case. See [Request](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awssupport/latest/user/create-service-quota-increase.html) a service quota [increase](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/awssupport/latest/user/create-service-quota-increase.html) in the *AWS Support User Guide*.

| Name                                                  | Default | Adjustabl<br>e | Comments                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
|-------------------------------------------------------|---------|----------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Prefix<br>lists<br>per<br>Region                      | 100     | Yes            |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
| Versions<br>per<br>prefix<br>list                     | 1,000   | Yes            | If<br>a<br>prefix<br>list<br>has<br>1,000<br>stored<br>versions<br>and<br>you<br>add<br>a<br>new<br>version,<br>the<br>oldest<br>version<br>is<br>removed<br>so<br>that<br>the<br>new<br>version<br>can<br>be<br>added.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |
| Maximum number of<br>entries<br>per<br>prefix<br>list | 1,000   | Yes            | You<br>can<br>resize<br>a<br>customer-managed<br>prefix<br>list<br>up<br>to<br>1000.<br>For<br>more<br>information, see Resize<br>a<br>prefix<br>list.<br>When<br>you<br>reference<br>a<br>prefix<br>list<br>in<br>a<br>resource,<br>the<br>maximum<br>number<br>of<br>entries<br>for<br>the<br>prefix<br>lists<br>counts<br>against<br>the quota for the number of entries for<br>the<br>resource.<br>For<br>example,<br>if<br>you<br>create<br>a<br>prefix<br>list<br>with<br>20<br>maximum<br>entries |

| Name                                                               | Default | Adjustabl<br>e | Comments                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
|--------------------------------------------------------------------|---------|----------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
|                                                                    |         |                | and<br>you<br>reference<br>that<br>prefix<br>list<br>in<br>a<br>security<br>group<br>rule,<br>this<br>counts<br>as<br>20<br>security<br>group<br>rules.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |
| References<br>to<br>a<br>prefix<br>list<br>per<br>resource<br>type | 10,000  | Yes            | This<br>quota<br>applies<br>per<br>resource<br>type<br>that<br>can<br>reference<br>a<br>prefix<br>list.<br>For<br>example,<br>you<br>can<br>have<br>10,000<br>reference<br>s<br>to<br>a<br>prefix<br>list<br>across<br>all<br>of<br>your<br>security<br>groups<br>plus<br>10,000<br>references<br>to<br>a<br>prefix<br>list<br>across<br>all<br>of<br>your<br>subnet<br>route<br>tables.<br>If<br>you<br>share<br>a<br>prefix<br>list<br>with<br>other<br>AWS<br>accounts,<br>the<br>other<br>accounts'<br>references<br>to<br>your<br>prefix<br>list<br>count<br>toward<br>your<br>quota. |

# <span id="page-680-0"></span>**Network ACLs**

| Name                           | Default | Adjustabl<br>e | Comments                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
|--------------------------------|---------|----------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Network<br>ACLs<br>per<br>VPC  | 200     | Yes            | You<br>can<br>associate<br>one<br>network<br>ACL<br>to<br>one<br>or<br>more<br>subnets<br>in<br>a<br>VPC.                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |
| Rules<br>per<br>network<br>ACL | 20      | Yes            | This quota determines both the<br>maximum number of inbound rules and<br>the maximum number of outbound<br>rules.<br>This<br>quota<br>can<br>be<br>increased<br>up<br>to a maximum of 40 inbound rules and<br>40 outbound rules (for a total of 80<br>rules),<br>but<br>network<br>performance<br>might<br>be<br>impacted. |

| Network<br>interfaces<br>per<br>Region   | 5,000                            | Yes            | This<br>quota<br>applies<br>to<br>individual<br>AWS<br>account<br>VPCs<br>and<br>shared<br>VPCs.<br>This<br>limit<br>is<br>enforced<br>per<br>Availability<br>Zone<br>(AZ).<br>If,<br>for<br>example,<br>the<br>network<br>interfaces<br>are<br>in<br>three<br>AZs,<br>each<br>AZ<br>will<br>have<br>a<br>limit<br>of<br>5,000<br>limit<br>and<br>the<br>Region<br>will<br>have<br>a<br>limit<br>of<br>15,000. |

| Inbound or outbound rules<br>per<br>security<br>group | 60      | Yes            | This<br>quota<br>is<br>enforced<br>separately<br>for<br>inbound<br>and<br>outbound<br>rules.<br>For<br>an<br>account<br>with<br>the<br>default<br>quota<br>of<br>60<br>rules,<br>a<br>security<br>group<br>can<br>have<br>60<br>inbound rules and 60 outbound rules.<br>In<br>addition,<br>this<br>quota<br>is<br>enforced<br>separately for IPv4 rules and IPv6 rules.<br>For<br>an<br>account<br>with<br>the<br>default<br>quota<br>of<br>60<br>rules,<br>a<br>security<br>group<br>can<br>have<br>60 inbound rules for IPv4 traffic and<br>60<br>inbound<br>rules<br>for<br>IPv6<br>traffic.<br>For<br>more<br>information,<br>see<br>the<br>section<br>called<br>"Security<br>group<br>size". |

# <span id="page-685-0"></span>**VPC subnet sharing**

All standard VPC quotas apply to shared VPC subnets.

| Name                                  | Default | Adjustabl<br>e | Comments                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
|---------------------------------------|---------|----------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Participant<br>accounts<br>per<br>VPC | 100     | Yes            | The<br>maximum<br>number<br>of<br>distinct<br>participant<br>accounts<br>that<br>subnets<br>in<br>a<br>VPC<br>can<br>be<br>shared<br>with.<br>This<br>is<br>a<br>per<br>VPC<br>quota<br>and<br>applies<br>across<br>all<br>the<br>subnets<br>shared<br>in<br>a<br>VPC.<br>VPC<br>owners<br>can<br>view<br>the<br>network<br>interfaces<br>and<br>security<br>groups<br>that<br>are<br>attached<br>to<br>the<br>participant<br>resources. |

VPC subnet sharing 675

| Name                                                            | Default | Adjustabl<br>e | Comments                                                                                              |
|-----------------------------------------------------------------|---------|----------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Subnets<br>that<br>can<br>be<br>shared<br>with<br>an<br>account | 100     | Yes            | This is the maximum number of subnets<br>that<br>can<br>be<br>shared<br>with<br>an<br>AWS<br>account. |

# <span id="page-686-0"></span>**Network Address Usage**

Network Address Usage (NAU) is comprised of IP addresses, network interfaces, and CIDRs in managed prefix lists. NAU is a metric applied to resources in a VPC to help you plan for and monitor the size of your VPC. For more information, see [Network](#page-138-0) Address Usage.

The resources that make up the NAU count have their own individual service quotas. Even if a VPC has NAU capacity available, you won't be able to launch resources into the VPC if the resources have exceeded their service quotas.

| Name                                  | Default | Adjustabl<br>e               | Comments                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
|---------------------------------------|---------|------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| Network<br>Address<br>Usage           | 64,000  | Yes (up<br>to to<br>256,000) | The<br>maximum<br>number<br>of<br>NAU<br>units<br>per<br>VPC.                                                                                                                                                                                          |
| Peered<br>Network<br>Address<br>Usage | 128,000 | Yes<br>(up to<br>512,000)    | The<br>maximum<br>number<br>of<br>NAU<br>units<br>for<br>a<br>VPC<br>and<br>all<br>of<br>its<br>intra-Region<br>peered<br>VPCs.<br>VPCs<br>that<br>are<br>peered<br>across<br>different<br>Regions<br>do<br>not<br>contribute<br>to<br>this<br>number. |

# <span id="page-686-1"></span>**Amazon EC2 API throttling**

For information about Amazon EC2 throttling, see Request [throttling](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/ec2/latest/devguide/ec2-api-throttling.html) in the *Amazon EC2 Developer Guide*.

Network Address Usage 676

# <span id="page-687-0"></span>**Additional quota resources**

For more information, see the following:

- AWS Client VPN [quotas](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/vpn/latest/clientvpn-admin/limits.html) in the *AWS Client VPN Administrator Guide*
- Direct [Connect](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/directconnect/latest/UserGuide/limits.html) quotas in the *AWS Direct Connect User Guide*
- [Peering](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/vpc/latest/peering/vpc-peering-connection-quotas.html) quotas in the *Amazon VPC Peering Guide*
- [PrivateLink quotas](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/vpc/latest/privatelink/vpc-limits-endpoints.html) in the *AWS PrivateLink Guide*
- [Site-to-Site VPN quotas](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/vpn/latest/s2svpn/vpn-limits.html) in the *AWS Site-to-Site VPN User Guide*
- Traffic [Mirroring](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/vpc/latest/mirroring/traffic-mirroring-quotas.html) quotas in the *Amazon VPC Traffic Mirroring Guide*
- Transit [gateway](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/vpc/latest/tgw/transit-gateway-quotas.html) quotas in the *Amazon VPC Transit Gateways Guide*

Additional quota resources 677

# <span id="page-688-0"></span>**Document history**

The following table describes the important changes in each release of the *Amazon VPC User Guide*.

| Change                                                                | Description                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         | Date                    |
|-----------------------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------|
| VPC<br>encryption<br>controls                                         | You<br>can<br>now<br>enforce<br>encryption<br>in<br>transit<br>for<br>network<br>traffic<br>within<br>your<br>VPC<br>using<br>VPC<br>encryption<br>controls.<br>This<br>feature<br>provides<br>centralized<br>encryption<br>policy<br>enforcement<br>and<br>monitoring<br>capabilities.                                                                             | November<br>21,<br>2025 |
| Regional<br>NAT<br>gateways<br>for<br>automatic multi-AZ expansion    | You<br>can<br>now<br>use<br>regional<br>NAT<br>gateways<br>that<br>automatic<br>ally<br>expand<br>across<br>Availabil<br>ity<br>Zones<br>based<br>on<br>your<br>workload<br>footprint.<br>Regional<br>NAT<br>gateways<br>provide<br>simplified<br>setup,<br>enhanced<br>security,<br>and<br>automatic<br>high<br>availability<br>without<br>manual<br>intervention. | November<br>19,<br>2025 |
| Route<br>inbound<br>VPC<br>traffic<br>to<br>public<br>IP<br>addresses | You<br>can<br>now<br>configure<br>advanced<br>routing<br>rules<br>to<br>direct<br>inbound<br>traffic<br>from<br>the internet to specific public<br>IP<br>addresses<br>within<br>your<br>VPC,<br>enabling<br>more<br>granular<br>control<br>over<br>traffic<br>flow<br>and<br>routing<br>decisions<br>for<br>ingress<br>scenarios.                                   | August 13, 2025         |

[Dynamic](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/vpc/latest/userguide/dynamic-routing-route-server.html) routing in your VPC using [Amazon](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/vpc/latest/userguide/dynamic-routing-route-server.html) VPC Route [Server](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/vpc/latest/userguide/dynamic-routing-route-server.html)

Amazon VPC Route Server simplifies routing for traffic between workloads that are deployed within a VPC and its internet gateways. With this feature, VPC Route Server dynamically updates VPC and gateway route tables with your preferred IPv4 or IPv6 routes to achieve routing fault tolerance for those workloads. This enables you to automatically reroute traffic within a VPC, which increases the manageability of VPC routing and interoper ability with third-party workloads.

March 31, 2025

AWS [managed](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/vpc/latest/userguide/security-iam-awsmanpol.html#security-iam-awsmanpol-updates) policy update Amazon VPC updated the AmazonVPCFullAccess and AmazonVPCReadOnlyAccess managed policies.

December 9, 2024

[Declarative](https://docs.aws.amazon.com//vpc/latest/userguide/security-vpc-bpa.html#security-vpc-bpa-exclusions-orgs) policy support for [VPC](https://docs.aws.amazon.com//vpc/latest/userguide/security-vpc-bpa.html#security-vpc-bpa-exclusions-orgs) BPA

If you are using AWS Organizations to manage accounts in your organizat ion, you can use a declarative policy to enforce VPC BPA on the accounts in the organizat ion.

December 1, 2024

| VPC<br>Block<br>Public<br>Access<br>(BPA)    | VPC<br>Block<br>public<br>Access<br>(BPA)<br>enables<br>you<br>to<br>block<br>resources<br>in<br>VPCs<br>and<br>subnets<br>that<br>you<br>own<br>in<br>a<br>Region<br>from<br>reaching<br>or<br>being<br>reached<br>from<br>the<br>internet<br>through<br>internet<br>gateways<br>and<br>egress<br>only<br>internet<br>gateways. | November<br>19,<br>2024 |
|----------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------|
| Shared<br>Security<br>Groups                 | This<br>feature<br>enables<br>you<br>to<br>share<br>a<br>security<br>group<br>with<br>other<br>AWS<br>Organizations<br>accounts.                                                                                                                                                                                                 | October<br>30,<br>2024  |
| Security<br>Group<br>VPC<br>Associati<br>ons | This<br>feature<br>enables<br>you<br>to<br>associate<br>a<br>security<br>group<br>with multiple VPCs in the<br>same<br>Region.                                                                                                                                                                                                   | October<br>30,<br>2024  |
| NAT<br>gateway<br>MTU<br>support             | NAT<br>gateways<br>support<br>traffic<br>with a maximum transmission<br>unit (MTU) of 8500.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      | September 10, 2024      |
| Private<br>IPv6<br>addressing                | Information about private<br>IPv6<br>addressing<br>was<br>added.<br>Private<br>IPv6<br>addresses<br>are<br>only<br>available<br>in<br>Amazon<br>VPC<br>IP<br>Address<br>Manager.                                                                                                                                                 | August 8, 2024          |
| IPv6<br>preferred<br>lease<br>time           | You<br>can<br>now<br>choose<br>how<br>frequently<br>a<br>running<br>insta<br>nce<br>with<br>an<br>IPv6<br>assigned<br>to<br>it<br>goes<br>through<br>DHCPv6<br>lease<br>renewal.                                                                                                                                                 | February<br>20,<br>2024 |

| Guide<br>structure<br>review<br>and<br>improvements | The<br>structure<br>of<br>the<br>guide<br>was<br>reviewed<br>and<br>improveme<br>nts<br>were<br>made<br>to<br>improve<br>the<br>customer<br>experience<br>related<br>to finding info for specific<br>scenarios.                                                                                                                                             | February<br>20,<br>2024 |
|-----------------------------------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------|-------------------------|
| AWS<br>managed<br>policy<br>update                  | Amazon<br>VPC<br>updated<br>the<br>AmazonVPCFullAccess<br>and<br>AmazonVPCReadOnlyAccess<br>managed policies.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               | February<br>8,<br>2024  |
| AWS<br>managed<br>policy<br>update                  | Amazon<br>VPC<br>updated<br>the<br>AmazonVPCCrossAcco<br>untNetworkInterfaceOperatio<br>ns<br>managed<br>policy.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            | September 25, 2023      |
| EC2-Classic<br>is<br>deprecated                     | With<br>EC2-Classic,<br>EC2<br>instances<br>ran<br>in<br>a<br>single,<br>flat<br>network<br>shared<br>with<br>other<br>customers.<br>Amazon<br>VPC<br>replaces<br>EC2-Classic.<br>With<br>Amazon<br>VPC,<br>your<br>instances<br>run<br>in<br>a<br>virtual<br>private<br>cloud<br>(VPC)<br>that's<br>logically<br>isolated<br>to<br>your<br>AWS<br>account. | July 31, 2023           |

| Add<br>secondary<br>IPv4<br>addresses |
|---------------------------------------|
| to<br>NAT<br>gateways                 |

You can add secondary private IPv4 addresses to public and private NAT gateways. Secondary IPv 4 addresses increase the number of available ports, and therefore they increase the limit on the number of concurrent connections that your workloads can establish using a NAT gateway.

January 31, 2023

# [Aligning with IAM best](#page-688-0) [practices](#page-688-0)

Updated guide to align with the IAM best practices . For more information, see Security best [practices](https://docs.aws.amazon.com//IAM/latest/UserGuide/best-practices.html) in IAM. January 4, 2023

# Pick the private IP [address](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/vpc/latest/userguide/vpc-nat-gateway.html#nat-gateway-creating) of your NAT [gateway](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/vpc/latest/userguide/vpc-nat-gateway.html#nat-gateway-creating)

When you create a NAT gateway, you can now choose to pick the private IP address that's assigned to the NAT gateway. Previousl y, the private IP address was automatically assigned from the IP address range of the subnet.

November 17, 2022

# IPv6 default [gateway](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/vpc/latest/userguide/configure-subnets.html#subnet-sizing) router [configuration](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/vpc/latest/userguide/configure-subnets.html#subnet-sizing)

Three IPv6 addresses are now reserved for use by the default VPC router.

November 11, 2022

Transfer Elastic IP [addresses](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/vpc/latest/userguide/vpc-eips.html#transfer-EIPs-intro) You can now transfer Elastic IP addresses from one AWS account to another.

October 31, 2022

VPC Flow Logs [delivery](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/vpc/latest/userguide/flow-logs-s3.html) options to [Amazon](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/vpc/latest/userguide/flow-logs-s3.html) S3 You can specify the Apache Parquet log file format, hourly partitions, and Hivecompatible S3 prefixes. October 13, 2021 [Amazon](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AWSEC2/latest/UserGuide/Using_Filtering.html#global-view) EC2 Global View Amazon EC2 Global View enables you to view VPCs, subnets, instances, security groups, and volumes across multiple AWS Regions in a single console. September 1, 2021 More [specific](#page-688-0) routes You can add a route to your route tables that is more specific than the local route. You can use more specific routes to redirect traffic between subnets within a VPC (East-West traffic) to a middlebox appliance. You can set the destination of a route to match an entire IPv4 or IPv6 CIDR block of a subnet in your VPC. August 30, 2021 [Resource](#page-688-0) IDs and tagging You can refer to security July 7, 2021

[support](#page-688-0) for security group [rules](#page-688-0)

group rules by resource ID. You can also add tags to your security group rules.

Private NAT [gateways](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/vpc/latest/userguide/vpc-nat-gateway.html) You can use a private NAT gateway for outbound-only private communication between VPCs or between a VPC and your on-premises network.

DNS [resolution](#page-688-0) support for [non-RFC](#page-688-0) 1918 IP address [ranges](#page-688-0)

The Amazon DNS server can now resolve private DNS hostnames to private IP addresses for all address spaces.

October 24, 2016

NAT [gateways](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/vpc/latest/userguide/vpc-nat-gateway.html) You can create a NAT gateway in a public subnet and enable instances in a private subnet to initiate outbound traffic to the internet or other AWS services.

December 17, 2015

VPC [flow](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/vpc/latest/userguide/flow-logs.html) logs You can create a flow log to capture information about the IP traffic going to and from network interfaces in your VPC.

June 10, 2015

[ClassicLink](#page-688-0) You can use ClassicLink to link

your EC2-Classic instance to a VPC in your account. You can associate VPC security groups with the EC2-Classic instance, enabling communica tion between your EC2-Class ic instance and instances in your VPC using private IP addresses.

in your VPC using custom DNS domain names that you define in a private hosted

November 5, 2014

zone in Route 53.
